local_names.romanized_local: Le Zhi Bing Gan (Chinese), Biskut Ritz (Malay), Rits Biscut (Tamil romanized), Ritz Biscuit (Hindi romanized)

region_of_origin: USA (Nabisco, launched 1934; owned by Mondelez International; distributed in Singapore by Mondelez Singapore Pte Ltd)
food_subgroup: Crackers and biscuits — packaged buttery cracker, canola oil base, 5 crackers = 16g label serving

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn URI exists for Ritz Crackers Original. Closest parent would be a generic cracker/biscuit class; URI not assigned to avoid fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A0HAD is the closest FoodEx2 parent for a commercial buttery cracker; no exact code for Ritz brand product.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this packaged cracker product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One label serving is 5 crackers (approximately 16g); a realistic snack portion is 10–15 crackers (32–48g).

nutrient_source.title: Ritz Crackers Original — USDA Branded FoodData Central FDC ID 175175 cross-referenced with Mondelez Singapore product label
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (Branded Foods) + Mondelez Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values per 100g derived from USDA Branded FDC 175175 (Ritz Crackers Original) and confirmed against Mondelez Singapore product label. USDA Branded Foods is an approved secondary global source for packaged/commercial products.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of refined wheat crackers — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for plain wheat crackers and refined starch snack biscuits
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for Ritz Crackers Original in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI ~70 is an analogous estimate based on published GI values for refined white wheat crackers and plain salted crackers (GI range 67–72) from the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per label serving (16g): available carbohydrate ~10g × GI 70 / 100 = GL ~7.0. At realistic serving (32g): GL ~14. At full sleeve (~51g): GL ~22.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content 813mg per 100g; ~130mg per label serving (5 crackers, 16g); ~260–390mg per realistic snack serving (10–15 crackers, 32–48g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Ritz Crackers to the label serving of 5 crackers (16g, ~130mg sodium) and avoid pairing with other high-sodium foods such as processed cheese or deli meats in the same meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High GI (~70) refined wheat cracker with zero dietary fibre, providing no glycaemic moderation; GL ~7 per label serving but ~14–22 at realistic consumption
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should restrict Ritz Crackers to the 5-cracker label serving and pair with a protein or fat source (e.g., hummus or peanut butter) to blunt the glycaemic response, as the absence of dietary fibre means no intrinsic glycaemic moderation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Canola oil base gives lower saturated fat (3.1g per 100g; ~0.5g per label serving) than palm-oil crackers, but high sodium (813mg/100g) and ultra-processed NOVA Class 4 formulation are cardiovascular concerns at higher consumption
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For cardiovascular disease management, Ritz Crackers Original is preferable to palm-oil-based crackers due to lower saturated fat, but sodium intake must be monitored and consumption should be limited to the label serving to avoid cumulative sodium load.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Canola oil provides a favourable MUFA profile with minor anti-inflammatory advantage; vitamin E ~2.5mg per 100g from canola oil
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Ritz Crackers Original made with canola oil offers a marginally better fatty acid profile than palm-oil crackers for rheumatoid arthritis patients, though the benefit is small and the product should still be consumed in moderation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ritz Crackers Original is a commercially manufactured, ultra-processed (NOVA Class 4) round buttery cracker produced by Nabisco (now Mondelez International) and distributed in Singapore by Mondelez Singapore Pte Ltd. The product is made from enriched wheat flour and canola oil; the characteristic buttery flavour is artificial (diacetyl-based) — Ritz Original contains no butter or dairy. The label serving is 5 crackers (~16g), providing approximately 80 kcal, 4g fat (saturated fat ~0.5g), 10g carbohydrate, and ~130mg sodium. Per 100g: 500 kcal, 25g fat, 3.1g saturated fat, 62.5g carbohydrate, 7.5g sugar, 0g dietary fibre, 813mg sodium. GI is approximately 70 (high), based on analogous refined wheat cracker data from the University of Sydney GI Database; GL per label serving is ~7 (low-moderate), rising to ~14–22 at realistic snack consumption of 10–15 crackers. The absence of dietary fibre means no intrinsic glycaemic buffering. Compared to palm-oil-based cream crackers, Ritz Original has significantly lower saturated fat (~0.5g vs ~2.4g per 16g serving). Clinically relevant for Type 2 Diabetes (high GI, zero fibre — pair with protein/fat to moderate glycaemic impact), Hypertension (sodium accumulates rapidly at realistic serving sizes), and Cardiovascular Disease (lower sat fat than alternatives but ultra-processed and sodium-dense). Source: USDA Branded FoodData Central FDC ID 175175 + Mondelez Singapore product label.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA Branded FoodData Central FDC ID 175175 (exact product match) and cross-referenced with Mondelez Singapore product label. Both are approved sources under Project Veritas v2 source policy. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for refined wheat crackers (confidence 0.9).
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown (MUFA/PUFA split) not reported in USDA Branded FDC 175175 for this product entry as of 2026-04-14. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in USDA Branded FDC 175175 for this branded cracker entry. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central FDC 175175 and Mondelez Singapore label. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central FDC 175175; refined wheat flour cracker with no added fibre. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero; product contains no animal-derived fat (canola oil base, no butter or dairy). vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_b12_mcg are 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central FDC 175175; no fortification for these nutrients in Ritz Original Singapore formulation. GI ~70 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for plain refined wheat crackers; no direct Ritz Crackers GI entry exists in the database. Glycemic load calculated as: available carbohydrate per serving (g) × GI / 100; label serving 16g = ~10g available carbohydrate × 70/100 = GL 7.0. NOVA classification: Class 4 (ultra-processed) — artificial buttery flavouring, emulsifiers, leavening agents. Product is dairy-free despite buttery flavour descriptor.
